## Fairshare & QOS {.smaller}

Slurm calculates a **priority score** per job — weighted by **Fairshare**, **QOS**, age, and partition.

**Fairshare** — users who consumed *less* than their allocated share get a priority boost; heavy users are penalised.

```bash
sshare -u $USER      # FairShare near 1.0 = under-used (↑ priority); near 0.0 = over-used (↓ priority)
sprio -j <job_id>    # show full priority breakdown
```

**Quality of Service (QOS)** — named policies set by admins that cap or boost jobs:

| Parameter | Effect |
|---|---|
| `MaxWall` | Maximum walltime allowed |
| `MaxTRESPerUser` | Cap on CPUs / GPUs / memory |
| `Priority` | Additive priority boost |
| `Preempt` | Can this QOS preempt others? |

```bash
sacctmgr show assoc user=$USER format=Account,QOS   # which QOS you can use
```

```bash
#SBATCH --qos=high_priority
```

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Why is my job still waiting?
High recent usage lowers your Fairshare score. Use `sprio -l` to see where your job ranks among all pending jobs.
:::
